text: The college has various clubs including the Sports Club, Fine Arts Club, Science Club, Tamil Mandram, Coding Club, Robotic Club, Energy and Eco Club, and Photography Club.
The MSAJCE Sports Club maintains records of sports events attended by students and submits annual reports on events, budget allocations, and spending.

1. To build up sportsmanship. 2. To foster friendships. 3. To reduce stress and improve health. 4. To enhance skills and employability. 5. To develop positive attitudes. 6. To experience community interaction. 7. To meet diverse individuals.
To encourage student involvement in sports activities at inter and intra college levels.
Teamwork Makes The Dream Work
The outdoor games conducted include Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, and Throughball.
The indoor games conducted include Carrom, Table Tennis, and Chess.
The ENVISTA CLUB aims to encourage student talent through skill-based training and artistic expression across various disciplines.
1. Encourage budding talent. 2. Provide opportunities for artistic expression. 3. Conduct various competitions and events. 4. Build confidence in a competitive landscape.
To inspire creativity and artistic expression through visual art forms.
The Science Club invites eminent scientists to inspire students and includes activities across Physics, Chemistry, and General science sectors.
Encourage problem-solving in relevant, science-based contexts through collaborative and hands-on activities.
Conducts seminars, workshops, exhibitions, and quiz competitions to promote knowledge in the field of science.
The Tamil Mandram aims to preserve the Tamil language and culture, inspiring pride among youth.
1. Develop enthusiasm for Tamil. 2. Spread ancient Tamil culture. 3. Conduct Tamil events and activities.
Conducts events including Irumugam Oru Agam (elocution/drama), Pessum Padam (storytelling), Aadu Puli (traditional game), and several others.
The Coding Club focuses on enhancing programming skills through contests, workshops, and open source initiatives.
To conduct coding contests, workshops on various computer science topics, and represent the college in intercollegiate competitions.
The Robotic Club provides a platform for students interested in robotics, promoting engagement in design and competition.
To encourage youth to become leaders in the robotics industry while developing practical engineering skills.
The Energy Club aims to educate students about sustainable energy use and conservation.

metadata.topic: Energy and Eco Club - Energy Club

text: The Eco Club empowers students to take action for environmental protection.

metadata.topic: Energy and Eco Club - Eco Club

text: The Photography Club encourages members to explore photography, filmmaking, and screenwriting.
Members develop skills in photography composition, editing, filmmaking, and event photography.
